**Indication:** Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)

## Patient dosing summary

| Patient ID | Age (months) | Weight (kg) | Dose (mg/kg) | Calculated dose (mg) | Frequency | Next dose | PK clearance (mL/min) | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| CU-001 | 34 | 12.4 | 0.15 | 1.86 | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 | Stable. Tumor regression 22% at last scan. |
| CU-002 | 18 | 9.1 | 0.12 | 1.09 | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 | Responding well. Weight gain improving. |
| CU-003 | 51 | 15.7 | 0.18 | 2.83 | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 | New enrollment. First dose administered 2026-03-27. |

## Quick assessment

- Current dosing parameters are present and internally consistent in the latest config.
- Patient notes do not indicate any immediate instability in response.
- Based on the config snapshot alone, no obvious PK-related dosing anomalies are apparent.
- A full stability assessment of the PK models would require checking recent model run logs and monitoring outputs in addition to the config file.
", recipients="")]Yes — I pulled the latest dosing config and prepared a summary report for Dr. Martinez.

Quick summary from `helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`:
- **Compound:** NXB-7042
- **Indication:** Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)
- **Last updated:** 2026-03-28

Patient dosing:
- **CU-001:** 12.4 kg × 0.15 mg/kg = **1.86 mg**, **q3w**, next dose **2026-04-14**, PK clearance **3.2 mL/min**
- **CU-002:** 9.1 kg × 0.12 mg/kg = **1.09 mg**, **q3w**, next dose **2026-04-12**, PK clearance **2.8 mL/min**
- **CU-003:** 15.7 kg × 0.18 mg/kg = **2.83 mg**, **q2w**, next dose **2026-04-10**, PK clearance **4.1 mL/min**

On PK stability:
- Based on the **current dosing config**, everything looks **generally stable**.
- The patient notes are reassuring:
  - CU-001: stable, 22% tumor regression
  - CU-002: responding well, weight improving
  - CU-003: new enrollment, first dose already administered
- That said, this is only a **config-level check**. I haven’t verified live model run logs or monitoring outputs, so I can’t fully certify end-to-end PK model stability from this file alone.

If you want, I can also format this into a shorter email-ready note for Dr. Martinez.
